rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('first preference-trained image-level router') and an implicit gap statement (GT-free supervision). Any meaningful review must reconstruct and evaluate whether the thesis, gap framing, and contribution claim are coherent and accurately stated.
rationale.R1.2: The method section asserts that inter-detector disagreement is a valid proxy for per-image difficulty; the results section then claims near-parity with a heavy detector. The inferential chain from proxy signal → preference loss → routing quality → benchmark claims requires logical consistency checking across all four paragraphs.
rationale.R1.3: The results paragraph makes precise quantitative assertions (53.4 mAP at 38% FLOPs; 0.4 mAP gap on LVIS at 30% cost; 184ms→71ms latency) that directly underwrite the contribution claim. Verifying that stated numbers support the narrative framing is load-bearing: a mismatch here invalidates the delegation regardless of review type.
rationale.R1.4: The contribution summary explicitly uses 'first,' making novelty a testable claim. The background paragraph cites BranchyNet, SkipNet, MSDNet, and image-level routers for detection — whether the preference-training framing and the GT-free signal are genuinely uncharted in those lines of work must be assessed.
rationale.R1.6: A section review conventionally includes prose quality. The text is technically dense and some transitions (e.g., the jump from router architecture to evaluation setup) may need smoothing. Writing polish is expected at 0.7 but is not independently load-bearing.
rationale.R1.7: Four prior-work systems are named without inline citations (Viola-Jones, BranchyNet, SkipNet, MSDNet); detector names (RT-DETR-L, DINO-Swin-L) are used without attribution. Citation accuracy and coverage over the adaptive-inference and cascade-detector literature is a standard review responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this a technical/scientific review (primary focus on logical validity, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty assessment) or a writing/editorial review (primary focus on prose clarity, flow, and style)?
